items.title: Young tradie murdered after night out 16 years ago, coroner finds

children.content: A coroner has found the investigation into a Broome tradies' murder, which remains an enduring mystery, was mishandled by WA Police.

gallery.altText: Image of a photograph of Josh Warneke placed in front of a memorial in Broome, Western Australia.

thumbnail.altText: Image of a photograph of Josh Warneke placed in front of a memorial in Broome, Western Australia.

items.title: Mother of Perth girl shot dead in Pakistan 'in extreme shock'

children.content: A nine-year-old girl fatally shot by police who were pursuing armed robbers in Pakistan is remembered as happy and bubbly by the principal of her Perth school, as her family tries to deal with their "tragic loss".

image.altText: Selfie of a man and his daughter on a plane.

items.title: 'Worrying' surge in people injecting peptides in WA

children.content: Needle exchanges in Western Australia are reporting dramatic increases in people accessing their services for peptide use.

image.altText: A collection of small clear vials containing different coloured liquid.

items.title: WA travellers among those owed $4.3 million as dream holidays shattered

children.content: Perth retiree Paul Beard is just one of the Australians left thousands of dollars out of pocket after the collapse of AVG Travels, with the situation prompting calls for more protection for travel agency customers.

image.altText: Paul Beard holds a drink in one hand.

items.title: Rising star Connolly fights cramp to hit stunning ton in Aussie victory

children.content: Cooper Connolly fights off his body going into shock due to cramps to record his maiden international century.

image.altText: Cooper Connolly raises his bat and helmet and smiles.

items.title: Picturesque islands in WA's north set to become transport hubs after $20m sale

children.content: A Kimberley iron ore mine with idyllic views across a tropical sea will close after 70 years of operation as a new phase begins for offshore oil and gas.

gallery.altText: aerial of a bright blue ocean and a green red island

thumbnail.altText: aerial of a bright blue ocean and a green red island

items.title: New watchdog proposed to probe behaviour of WA judges

children.content: Complaints about the behaviour and capacity of judges and magistrates will be investigated by an independent watchdog, under new legislation to be introduced to WA parliament this week.

gallery.altText: A judge in black robes writes on white paper in a court room with a gavel in front of them.

thumbnail.altText: A judge in black robes writes on white paper in a court room with a gavel in front of them.

items.title: China's ability to hit Australia with missiles 'real and growing'

children.content: New analysis warns that China's military is developing a "real and growing" ability to hit the Australian mainland with missiles, and can already threaten Australia's trade routes, subsea cables and critical infrastructure.

image.altText: Military vehicles carrying DF-26 ballistic missiles during a military parade in China.

items.title: New doco details how Rolf Harris was able to hide his crimes

children.content: Plus Brisbane is a main character in a new rom-com, Russell T Davies delivers a devastating drama, and we bring you more shows lighting up TV this month.

image.altText: Rolf Harris as a young man standing on a busy city street wearing a large ornate crown

items.title: FIFA's hydration break farce the ultimate insult for weary World Cup fans

children.content: Football fans are forced to endure much to keep their love of the beautiful game alive, but FIFA's cynical new "hydration breaks" are stretching the friendship like never before.

articles.title: Quick hits: Curaçao's special goal and De Oranje brighten up day four

children.content: De Oranje hit the World Cup and in the middle of a 7-1 thumping, Curaçao's lone goal might have meant the most of all. Here are today's quick hits.

articles.title: Sweden's Premier League stars shine to cast Tunisia aside

children.content: Sweden dispatches Tunisia after Côte d'Ivoire scores a last-minute winner against Ecuador and Japan finds a late leveller against the Netherlands.

image.altText: Alexander Isak and Viktor Gyokeres run off in celebration arm in arm

articles.title: 'Let them talk': Socceroos hero bites back at claim Türkiye 'dominated'

children.content: Socceroos goalscoring hero Nestory Irankunda says Türkiye could not back up its talk, despite one of its stars claiming post-game Australia had been "dominated".

image.altText: Nestory Irankunda looks at the ball in front of his face during a game

gallery.altText: Socceroos players have a drink during the game against Türkiye

thumbnail.altText: Socceroos players have a drink during the game against Türkiye

items.title: Woman ditches custom wedding gown for $90 op shop dream dress

children.content: Kaila Scally-Chadwick abandoned her previously purchased made-to-order gown when she found her dream dress in a second-hand shop just months out from the big day.

image.altText: Baby wearing white standing holding the train of a lace wedding dress and looking at the camera with mum crouched beside

items.title: A lost Sam Pang went looking for something. He found comedy

children.content: This workplace satire starring Sam Pang has Tasmania's first ever AFL team firmly in its sights.

image.altText: Sam Pang wears a suit and sits on a stool for a studio style portrait

items.title: Former potato farm given new life as bird sanctuary

children.content: Once drained for farm irrigation, this former potato farm now attracts birds from as far away as Siberia.

gallery.altText: An aerial shot of a wetlands area.

thumbnail.altText: An aerial shot of a wetlands area.

items.title: History quiz: Test yourself on these past events

children.content: When was Salt Lake City awarded the XIX Winter Olympics? And how much do you know about the Fast and the Furious films? Test yourself in this week's history quiz.

image.altText: Frida Kahlo, Mexico, art, artist, anniversary, self-portrait

items.title: 'Absolutely terrified': Scolyer's doctor reveals fears of experimental therapy

children.content: Just 11 weeks before he died, Richard Scolyer cycled 500km in four days. In his last major TV interview, the celebrated pathologist tells Australian Story the secret to a fulfilling life and his doctor reveals the huge risks he took to try new brain cancer treatment.

image.altText: Man in a blue-collared button-up shirt sits in a dining room chair, a neutral facial expression, a lamp in background

items.title: People delight in finding wildflowers all year round in WA

children.content: WA has a reputation for putting on incredible wildflower displays, with those on the ground saying you can find something special all year round.

gallery.altText: A close of a purple flower

thumbnail.altText: A close of a purple flower

items.title: Business groups urge rejection of CGT changes

children.content: Business groups are urging the government to scrap its capital gains tax changes as the first tranche of measures is scrutinised at a parliamentary inquiry.

image.altText: Jim Chalmers close up looking serious with Australian flag in background.

items.title: Is SpaceX worth $3 trillion? Of course not, but that's not the point

children.content: To get a sense of the difference between the Tesla and SpaceX IPOs, consider that 1.7 billion seconds ago was 1972, when Elton John released Rocket Man, while 1.7 trillion seconds ago was 52,000BC, when we shared the planet with Neanderthals.

image.altText: Elon Musk wearing a suit behind a dark backdrop.

avatar.alt: A man in a suit jacket wearing glasses

items.title: Soccer tournament unites refugees through universal language of sport

children.content: A soccer tournament in Perth's north helps participants overcome barriers, while inspiring more women to pursue the game, as part of Refugee Week.

Ladies with head scarves watch a soccer match.

items.title: Multi-billion-dollar economic policy undermined by 'black cladding'

children.content: Almost $15 billion has been spent over a decade via the federal government's Indigenous Procurement Policy, but critics say it's failed to make a significant difference to Indigenous people due to the practice of "black cladding".

image.altText: An Aboriginal flag flying outside of parliament house

items.title: The desperate measures young people are taking as they try to secure rentals

children.content: Young Australians are taking desperate measures to secure rental homes in an over-saturated market, which is leaving "more homeless than ever before".

image.altText: A 24-year-old woman and her dog looking at the camera

items.title: Locations released for 18 community batteries in Perth, Bunbury

children.content: Construction begins on 18 community batteries across WA, as a climate and conservation advocate calls for a legislated target for renewable energy uptake.
